mutators.name: recordActivity

params.name: input
params.type: { name: string; occurredAt: Instant; status: 'pending' | 'done'; assetHandles: readonly AssetHandle[]; notes?: string }

mutators.semantics: Appends one activity-log event, linked to all supplied assets, and returns its collision-free replica-scoped client id (prefix_replicaId~counter); the activity is a log event (isLog:true).

projections.name: listActivityLogsForAsset
projections.semantics: Returns activity-log views linked to asset and visible at-or-before asOf, optionally filtered by status, ordered newest occurredAt first with HLC as deterministic tie-breaker; limit truncates that order.

projections.name: getFirstActivityLogForAsset
projections.semantics: Returns the first (newest under the activity-log ordering) matching activity log linked to asset at-or-before asOf, or null when none exists.

rationale: The sole state-changing operation is recording an activity as an immutable domain event; associating multiple selected assets is atomic within that one event, matching the add-log action without a central multi-write transaction. ActivityLogHandle is an opaque replica-scoped client id using the bound rule `prefix_replicaId~counter`. Both asset-log reads are materialized, time-bounded projections because later appended activity events can change their result. `getFirstActivityLogForAsset` is retained as the explicit source-level first-log semantic rather than requiring callers to infer it from list ordering. No quantity, movement, membership, or access surface is included because this feature inventory supplies no activity-log semantic requiring it.
